items.title: Claude's hidden inner monologue is now readable thanks to Anthropic's new Jacobian Lens
items.summary: Anthropic has found that Claude developed an internal working memory on its own during training. The company calls it "J-Space" and can now read it using a new analysis tool called J-Lens. The working memory reveals that Claude recognizes contrived test scenarios before producing

items.title: Chinese AI models regularly pass 30 percent on OpenRouter as cost gap widens
items.summary: Chinese AI models are gaining ground with US companies because they cost far less than systems from OpenAI and Anthropic, CNBC reports. The article Chinese AI models regularly pass 30 percent on OpenRouter as cost gap widens appeared first on The Decoder.

items.title: Deepseek is designing its own AI chip
items.summary: Chinese startup Deepseek is building its own AI chip, Reuters reports. The article Deepseek is designing its own AI chip appeared first on The Decoder.

items.title: OpenAI and Anthropic are giving away millions in computing power to attract startups
items.summary: OpenAI, Anthropic, and major cloud providers are racing to outbid each other with free compute credits to pull startups into their ecosystems. Some individual offers top $3 million. At Y Combinator alone, OpenAI and Anthropic could hand out up to $800 million in credits per year

items.title: Apollo economist warns AI profit gains outside tech could take "well beyond" what Wall Street expects

items.title: GPT-4's dominance lasted a year while today's top models barely survive seven weeks at the top
items.summary: OpenAI's GPT-4 led the Epoch Capabilities Index for about a year, far longer than any model since. Since Claude 3 Opus took the top spot in February 2024, the lead has changed hands 17 times, with a median stay of just seven weeks. Competition is fiercer now, but the capability g

items.title: Nvidia's Kyber NVL144 reportedly pushed back more than a year, Asian suppliers drop
items.summary: Nvidia's next AI server rack, Kyber NVL144, has been delayed more than a year to 2028 because of circuit board manufacturing problems, according to analyst firm SemiAnalysis. Asian suppliers lost up to double-digit percentages in market value. The more powerful Rubin Ultra varian

items.title: US investors will soon get access to SK Hynix, another memory maker riding the AI boom
items.summary: SK Hynix is experiencing a boom credited to AI. It will ride that to a multibillion-dollar U.S. IPO, expected to take place on Friday.

items.title: DeepSeek V4-Pro and V4-Flash hold the open-weight frontier
items.summary: DeepSeek's MIT-licensed MoE V4 models (1.6T-param Pro and 284B Flash, both 1M-token context) remain the leading open-weight challenge to closed frontier labs, with aggressive pricing ($0.87 per million output tokens) and strong agentic performance, keeping cost and openness pressure on US labs.
